questions.text: What is a nullable type in Kotlin?

answers.text: A type that cannot hold any value
A type that can hold null as a value
A type that is used for loops

correctAnswer.text: A type that can hold null as a value

questions.text: What is the entry point function in Kotlin?

answers.text: main
start
run

correctAnswer.text: main

questions.text: What is the keyword used to define a class in Kotlin?

answers.text: struct
class
type

correctAnswer.text: class

questions.text: What is a data class in Kotlin?

answers.text: A class used to store only primitive data types
A class that automatically generates equals, hashCode, and copy functions
A class used to create instances of other classes

correctAnswer.text: A class that automatically generates equals, hashCode, and copy functions

questions.text: What is the correct way to declare a read-only property in Kotlin?

answers.text: var property: String
const val property: String
val property: String

correctAnswer.text: val property: String

questions.text: What is the 'when' keyword used for in Kotlin?

answers.text: Looping over a collection
Declaring a function
Replacing a 'switch' statement

correctAnswer.text: Replacing a 'switch' statement

questions.text: What is the 'let' function used for in Kotlin?

answers.text: Declaring a new variable
Checking for null
Executing a block of code on a non-null object

correctAnswer.text: Executing a block of code on a non-null object

questions.text: What is the 'apply' function used for in Kotlin?

answers.text: Performing type conversion
Configuring properties of an object
Performing arithmetic operations

correctAnswer.text: Configuring properties of an object

questions.text: What is the Kotlin keyword used to declare an interface?

answers.text: interface
protocol
implement

correctAnswer.text: interface

questions.text: What is the 'lateinit' keyword used for in Kotlin?

answers.text: Specifying that a property can be modified
Specifying that a property will have a value assigned later
Specifying that a property cannot be modified

correctAnswer.text: Specifying that a property will have a value assigned later

questions.text: What is the Elvis operator in Kotlin?

answers.text: A way to define nullable properties
A way to perform safe casts
A shorthand for checking nullability and providing an alternative value

correctAnswer.text: A shorthand for checking nullability and providing an alternative value

questions.text: What is the purpose of the 'run' function in Kotlin?

answers.text: Performing a database query
Running a background thread
Executing a block of code on a non-null object and returning the result

correctAnswer.text: Executing a block of code on a non-null object and returning the result

questions.text: What is the correct syntax for defining a lambda function in Kotlin?

answers.text: { param -> body }
[param] => body
(param) => body

correctAnswer.text: { param -> body }

questions.text: What is the purpose of the 'also' function in Kotlin?

answers.text: Changing the value of a variable
Filtering elements in a list
Performing additional actions on an object and returning it

correctAnswer.text: Performing additional actions on an object and returning it

questions.text: Which symbol is used to make a class inherit from another class in Kotlin?

answers.text: extends

questions.text: What is the purpose of the 'repeat' function in Kotlin?

answers.text: Performing a calculation repeatedly
Repeating a block of code a specified number of times
Creating a new list by repeating elements

correctAnswer.text: Repeating a block of code a specified number of times

questions.text: What is the purpose of the 'by lazy' keyword in Kotlin?

answers.text: Defining a property with a default value
Creating a delegate property
Creating a lazily initialized property

correctAnswer.text: Creating a lazily initialized property

questions.text: What is the purpose of the 'runCatching' function in Kotlin?

answers.text: Catching runtime exceptions and returning a Result
Running code on the UI thread
Handling checked exceptions

correctAnswer.text: Catching runtime exceptions and returning a Result

questions.text: What is the correct way to create an instance of a class in Kotlin?

answers.text: val obj = MyClass()
MyClass obj = new MyClass()
val obj = new MyClass()

correctAnswer.text: val obj = MyClass()

questions.text: What is the purpose of the 'with' function in Kotlin?

answers.text: Performing an operation with null safety
Performing multiple operations with a single object
Performing a type conversion

correctAnswer.text: Performing multiple operations with a single object

questions.text: Which keyword is used to declare a coroutine in Kotlin?

answers.text: async
suspend
coroutine

correctAnswer.text: suspend

questions.text: What is a sealed class in Kotlin?

answers.text: A class that cannot be instantiated
A class whose subclasses are restricted to the same file
A class that cannot have properties

correctAnswer.text: A class whose subclasses are restricted to the same file

questions.text: How do you define an extension function in Kotlin?

answers.text: fun ClassName.functionName() {}
extend fun functionName() {}
fun functionName(this: ClassName) {}

correctAnswer.text: fun ClassName.functionName() {}

questions.text: What does the '?.' operator do in Kotlin?

answers.text: Throws an exception if the object is null
Calls a method only if the object is not null
Converts a nullable type to a non-null type

correctAnswer.text: Calls a method only if the object is not null

questions.text: What is the purpose of the 'object' keyword in Kotlin?

answers.text: To create an anonymous class or singleton
To declare a variable
To define an interface

correctAnswer.text: To create an anonymous class or singleton

questions.text: What is a companion object in Kotlin?

answers.text: An object that accompanies a coroutine
A singleton object tied to a class, similar to static members in Java
An object shared between two classes

correctAnswer.text: A singleton object tied to a class, similar to static members in Java

questions.text: Which collection function returns a new list with elements that match a condition?

answers.text: map
filter
reduce

correctAnswer.text: filter

questions.text: What does the 'map' function do on a collection in Kotlin?

answers.text: Filters elements based on a condition
Transforms each element and returns a new list
Sorts elements in ascending order

correctAnswer.text: Transforms each element and returns a new list

questions.text: What is the difference between 'List' and 'MutableList' in Kotlin?

answers.text: List allows duplicates, MutableList does not
List is read-only, MutableList allows modification
List is ordered, MutableList is unordered

correctAnswer.text: List is read-only, MutableList allows modification

questions.text: What does the '!!' operator do in Kotlin?

answers.text: Checks if a value is not null
Asserts that a value is non-null, throwing NPE if null
Negates a boolean value twice

correctAnswer.text: Asserts that a value is non-null, throwing NPE if null

questions.text: What is an enum class in Kotlin?

answers.text: A class that holds a fixed set of constants
A class that cannot be inherited
A class that iterates over values

correctAnswer.text: A class that holds a fixed set of constants

questions.text: How is a higher-order function defined in Kotlin?

answers.text: A function that takes another function as a parameter or returns one
A function that calls itself recursively
A function that runs on a higher-priority thread

correctAnswer.text: A function that takes another function as a parameter or returns one

questions.text: What does 'kotlinx.coroutines.launch' do?

answers.text: Launches a new coroutine without blocking the current thread
Launches an Android Activity
Starts a new background thread

correctAnswer.text: Launches a new coroutine without blocking the current thread

questions.text: What is the difference between 'launch' and 'async' in Kotlin coroutines?

answers.text: 'launch' returns a Deferred, 'async' returns a Job
'launch' returns a Job, 'async' returns a Deferred with a result
They are identical in behavior

correctAnswer.text: 'launch' returns a Job, 'async' returns a Deferred with a result

questions.text: What is 'Flow' in Kotlin?

answers.text: A cold asynchronous stream that emits multiple values
A way to store key-value pairs
A coroutine dispatcher

correctAnswer.text: A cold asynchronous stream that emits multiple values

questions.text: Which dispatcher should be used for CPU-intensive tasks in Kotlin coroutines?

answers.text: Dispatchers.Main
Dispatchers.IO
Dispatchers.Default

correctAnswer.text: Dispatchers.Default

questions.text: What is the use of 'Dispatchers.IO' in Kotlin coroutines?

answers.text: For UI updates on the main thread
For offloading blocking IO operations
For CPU-intensive computations

correctAnswer.text: For offloading blocking IO operations

questions.text: What is a generic type in Kotlin?

answers.text: A type that can only hold integer values
A type parameter that allows classes and functions to work with any type
A type used only in collections

correctAnswer.text: A type parameter that allows classes and functions to work with any type

questions.text: What does 'reified' mean in Kotlin generics?

answers.text: The type parameter can be accessed at runtime inside an inline function
The type parameter is restricted to numeric types
The type is converted to a string at compile time

correctAnswer.text: The type parameter can be accessed at runtime inside an inline function

questions.text: What is the 'inline' keyword used for in Kotlin?

answers.text: To make a function run faster by copying its body at the call site
To define a function inside another function
To prevent a function from being overridden

correctAnswer.text: To make a function run faster by copying its body at the call site

questions.text: How do you declare a constant at compile time in Kotlin?

answers.text: val CONSTANT = 10
const val CONSTANT = 10
static val CONSTANT = 10

correctAnswer.text: const val CONSTANT = 10

questions.text: What is the purpose of the 'open' keyword in Kotlin?

answers.text: To open a file for reading
To allow a class or function to be overridden or subclassed
To make a class abstract

correctAnswer.text: To allow a class or function to be overridden or subclassed

questions.text: What is the 'override' keyword used for in Kotlin?

answers.text: To prevent a method from being called
To provide a new implementation of a method from a superclass or interface
To declare a method as abstract

correctAnswer.text: To provide a new implementation of a method from a superclass or interface

questions.text: What is the 'abstract' keyword used for in Kotlin?

answers.text: To declare a class or method that must be implemented by subclasses
To prevent a class from having properties
To hide a class from other packages

correctAnswer.text: To declare a class or method that must be implemented by subclasses

questions.text: What does the 'forEach' function do on a collection?

answers.text: Returns the first element of the collection
Iterates over each element and performs an action
Creates a new collection from the elements

correctAnswer.text: Iterates over each element and performs an action

questions.text: What is the result of calling 'listOf()' in Kotlin?

answers.text: An empty mutable list
An empty immutable list
A null value

correctAnswer.text: An empty immutable list

questions.text: What is the use of 'mapOf()' in Kotlin?

answers.text: Creates a mutable map of key-value pairs
Creates an immutable map of key-value pairs
Maps a function over a list

correctAnswer.text: Creates an immutable map of key-value pairs

questions.text: What does the 'reduce' function do on a collection in Kotlin?

answers.text: Removes duplicate elements from a list
Accumulates a value by applying an operation to each element
Reduces the size of the list by half

correctAnswer.text: Accumulates a value by applying an operation to each element

questions.text: What is the difference between 'fold' and 'reduce' in Kotlin?

answers.text: 'fold' requires an initial value, 'reduce' uses the first element as the initial value
'fold' works only on numbers, 'reduce' works on all types
They are identical

correctAnswer.text: 'fold' requires an initial value, 'reduce' uses the first element as the initial value

questions.text: What is the 'Elvis operator' symbol in Kotlin?
What does the 'is' operator do in Kotlin?

answers.text: Checks if two objects are equal
Checks if an object is of a certain type
Converts an object to another type

correctAnswer.text: Checks if an object is of a certain type

questions.text: What is smart casting in Kotlin?

answers.text: Automatically converting types when the compiler can guarantee safety
A way to cast objects using generics
A casting mechanism that works at runtime only

correctAnswer.text: Automatically converting types when the compiler can guarantee safety

questions.text: How do you perform a safe cast in Kotlin?

answers.text: obj as? TargetType
obj cast TargetType
(TargetType) obj

correctAnswer.text: obj as? TargetType

questions.text: What is the 'takeIf' function used for in Kotlin?

answers.text: Returns the object if the predicate is true, otherwise null
Takes the first element from a list if it exists
Executes a block of code only if the condition is true

correctAnswer.text: Returns the object if the predicate is true, otherwise null

questions.text: What is the 'takeUnless' function used for in Kotlin?

answers.text: Returns the object if the predicate is false, otherwise null
Takes all elements unless a condition is met
Removes elements that do not match a predicate

correctAnswer.text: Returns the object if the predicate is false, otherwise null

questions.text: What is the difference between '==' and '===' in Kotlin?

answers.text: '==' checks structural equality, '===' checks referential equality
'==' checks referential equality, '===' checks structural equality
They are identical operators

correctAnswer.text: '==' checks structural equality, '===' checks referential equality

questions.text: What is a primary constructor in Kotlin?

answers.text: The first constructor defined in the class body
A constructor defined in the class header
A constructor that cannot have parameters

correctAnswer.text: A constructor defined in the class header

questions.text: What is the 'init' block used for in Kotlin?

answers.text: To initialize static properties
To run initialization code when an object is created
To define default parameter values

correctAnswer.text: To run initialization code when an object is created

questions.text: What is a secondary constructor in Kotlin?

answers.text: An additional constructor defined inside the class body using 'constructor'
A constructor inherited from the superclass
A constructor that runs after the primary constructor

correctAnswer.text: An additional constructor defined inside the class body using 'constructor'

questions.text: What is destructuring declaration in Kotlin?

answers.text: Breaking down an object's components into individual variables

Removing an object from memory

correctAnswer.text: Breaking down an object's components into individual variables

questions.text: What is a Kotlin 'object expression'?

answers.text: An expression that creates an anonymous object
An expression that evaluates to null
An expression used inside a companion object

questions.text: What is the 'typealias' keyword used for in Kotlin?

answers.text: Creating an alternative name for an existing type

Creating a new primitive type

correctAnswer.text: Creating an alternative name for an existing type

questions.text: What is the 'vararg' keyword used for in Kotlin?

answers.text: Declaring a variable that holds multiple values
Allowing a function to accept a variable number of arguments
Declaring a mutable variable argument

questions.text: What is the 'groupBy' function used for in Kotlin?

answers.text: Sorting elements into groups

Joining two lists together

questions.text: What is a Kotlin 'Sequence'?

answers.text: An ordered list of unique elements
A lazily evaluated collection that processes elements one at a time

questions.text: Which visibility modifier makes a declaration accessible only within the same file in Kotlin?

answers.text: private
internal
private (at top level)

questions.text: What does the 'internal' visibility modifier mean in Kotlin?

answers.text: Accessible only within the same class
Accessible within the same module
Accessible within the same package

correctAnswer.text: Accessible within the same module

questions.text: What is the 'super' keyword used for in Kotlin?

answers.text: To access members of the superclass
To call the main function
To declare a supertype

questions.text: What is the 'this' keyword used for in Kotlin?

answers.text: To refer to the current instance of the class
To call the superclass constructor
To declare a new object

questions.text: How do you define default parameter values in a Kotlin function?

answers.text: fun greet(name: String = "World") {}
fun greet(name: String := "World") {}
fun greet(default name: String) {}

correctAnswer.text: fun greet(name: String = "World") {}

questions.text: What are named arguments in Kotlin?

answers.text: Arguments that can only be used in constructors
Specifying argument names explicitly when calling a function
Arguments that are automatically named by the compiler

correctAnswer.text: Specifying argument names explicitly when calling a function

questions.text: What is a 'StateFlow' in Kotlin?

answers.text: A hot flow that holds a single updatable state value
A cold flow that emits a single value
A flow that stores a list of states

correctAnswer.text: A hot flow that holds a single updatable state value

questions.text: What is a 'SharedFlow' in Kotlin?

answers.text: A hot flow that broadcasts values to multiple collectors
A flow shared between two threads
A cold flow that can be shared across coroutines

correctAnswer.text: A hot flow that broadcasts values to multiple collectors

questions.text: What does 'withContext' do in Kotlin coroutines?

answers.text: Creates a new coroutine scope
Switches the coroutine context and suspends until the block completes
Cancels the current coroutine

correctAnswer.text: Switches the coroutine context and suspends until the block completes

questions.text: What does the 'sortedBy' function do in Kotlin?

answers.text: Sorts a collection in descending order
Returns a new list sorted by a given selector function
Sorts a collection in place

correctAnswer.text: Returns a new list sorted by a given selector function

questions.text: What is the 'count' function used for in Kotlin collections?

answers.text: Returns the number of elements, optionally matching a predicate
Counts the sum of all elements
Returns the index of an element

correctAnswer.text: Returns the number of elements, optionally matching a predicate

questions.text: What is operator overloading in Kotlin?

answers.text: Redefining how standard operators behave for custom types
Using multiple operators in a single expression
Overloading a function with the same name

correctAnswer.text: Redefining how standard operators behave for custom types

questions.text: What is the 'in' operator used for in Kotlin?

answers.text: Checking if a value exists in a range or collection
Iterating over an array
Importing a package

correctAnswer.text: Checking if a value exists in a range or collection

questions.text: What is a Kotlin 'Range'?

answers.text: A collection of random values
A sequence of values between a start and end point
A list with a fixed maximum size

correctAnswer.text: A sequence of values between a start and end point

questions.text: How do you create a range in Kotlin?

answers.text: range(1, 10)

questions.text: What is the 'downTo' function used for in Kotlin?

answers.text: Creating a range that counts downward
Moving to a lower coroutine priority
Reducing a list to a smaller size

correctAnswer.text: Creating a range that counts downward

questions.text: What does the 'step' keyword do in a Kotlin range?

answers.text: Defines the increment between values in the range
Skips elements that match a condition
Stops iteration at a certain value

correctAnswer.text: Defines the increment between values in the range

questions.text: What is a 'Set' in Kotlin?

answers.text: An ordered list of elements
A collection of unique elements with no guaranteed order
A mutable list with a fixed size

correctAnswer.text: A collection of unique elements with no guaranteed order

questions.text: What is the 'first' function used for in Kotlin collections?

answers.text: Returns the last element of the collection
Returns the first element, or throws if empty
Returns null if the collection is empty

correctAnswer.text: Returns the first element, or throws if empty

questions.text: What does 'firstOrNull' return when a collection is empty?

answers.text: Throws NoSuchElementException
Returns null
Returns a default value

correctAnswer.text: Returns null

questions.text: What is the 'any' function used for in Kotlin collections?

answers.text: Returns true if at least one element matches the predicate
Returns true if all elements match the predicate
Returns any random element from the collection

correctAnswer.text: Returns true if at least one element matches the predicate

questions.text: What is the 'all' function used for in Kotlin collections?

answers.text: Returns all elements as a new list
Returns true if all elements match the predicate
Returns the total count of the collection

correctAnswer.text: Returns true if all elements match the predicate

questions.text: What is the 'none' function used for in Kotlin collections?

answers.text: Returns true if no elements match the predicate
Removes all elements from a collection
Returns an empty collection

correctAnswer.text: Returns true if no elements match the predicate

questions.text: What is the 'zip' function used for in Kotlin?

answers.text: Compressing a collection to reduce its size
Combining two collections into a list of pairs
Merging two maps together

correctAnswer.text: Combining two collections into a list of pairs

questions.text: What is tail recursion in Kotlin?

answers.text: A recursive call made at the end of a function, optimized by the compiler to avoid stack overflow
A recursion that uses a loop internally
A function that calls itself from multiple points

correctAnswer.text: A recursive call made at the end of a function, optimized by the compiler to avoid stack overflow

questions.text: How do you enable tail recursion optimization in Kotlin?

answers.text: Use the 'tailrec' modifier before 'fun'
Use the 'recursive' keyword
Use '@TailRec' annotation

correctAnswer.text: Use the 'tailrec' modifier before 'fun'

questions.text: What is the 'delegate' pattern in Kotlin properties?

answers.text: Outsourcing get and set logic of a property to another object
Passing a property to a function as a parameter
Assigning a property to multiple variables

correctAnswer.text: Outsourcing get and set logic of a property to another object

questions.text: What is the 'observable' delegate in Kotlin?

answers.text: A property delegate that calls a handler whenever the value changes
A property that can only be read, not written
A delegate that logs all property accesses

correctAnswer.text: A property delegate that calls a handler whenever the value changes

questions.text: What is the purpose of the 'crossinline' modifier in Kotlin?

answers.text: Prevents non-local returns from lambdas passed to inline functions
Allows a lambda to cross between coroutine contexts
Restricts a lambda to a single call site

correctAnswer.text: Prevents non-local returns from lambdas passed to inline functions

questions.text: What is the 'noinline' modifier used for in Kotlin?

answers.text: Prevents a specific lambda parameter from being inlined in an inline function
Disables inlining for the entire function
Marks a lambda as non-executable

correctAnswer.text: Prevents a specific lambda parameter from being inlined in an inline function

questions.text: What is covariance in Kotlin generics?

answers.text: Allowing a generic type to be used as its supertype using 'out'
Restricting a generic type to a specific subtype using 'in'
A way to compare two generic types

correctAnswer.text: Allowing a generic type to be used as its supertype using 'out'

questions.text: What is contravariance in Kotlin generics?

answers.text: Allowing a generic type to accept supertypes using 'in'
Restricting a generic type to produce values using 'out'
Converting a type to its opposite nullability

correctAnswer.text: Allowing a generic type to accept supertypes using 'in'

questions.text: What is a value class in Kotlin?

answers.text: A class annotated with @JvmInline that wraps a single value with zero overhead at runtime
A class that only contains val properties
A class whose instances are always compared by value

correctAnswer.text: A class annotated with @JvmInline that wraps a single value with zero overhead at runtime

questions.text: What is the 'suspend' keyword's main purpose in Kotlin?

answers.text: To mark a function that can be paused and resumed without blocking a thread
To stop a coroutine permanently
To declare a function that runs in the background

correctAnswer.text: To mark a function that can be paused and resumed without blocking a thread
